alchemy_tips.mechanics: To make potions you need a Mortar and Pestle (minimum) plus 2-4 ingredients sharing at least one effect. Optional apparatus: Retort (boosts positive effects), Calcinator (increases all effect magnitude/duration), Alembic (reduces negative effects). Success chance = Alchemy skill + Intelligence/10 + Luck/10. Potion value = (Alchemy + Intelligence/10 + Luck/10) * Mortar quality. Effect visibility: 0-14 skill sees no effects, 15-29 sees 1st, 30-44 sees 2, 45-59 sees 3, 60+ sees all 4.

useful_potions.name: Restore Health
useful_potions.effect: Restore Health. Both are cheap and common, making this the most accessible healing potion.

useful_potions.name: Restore Fatigue
useful_potions.effect: Restore Fatigue. Abundant ingredients found in most towns and farms.

useful_potions.name: Fortify Intelligence
useful_potions.effect: Fortify Intelligence. Boosts alchemy skill indirectly, enabling the Intelligence-stacking exploit for extremely powerful potions.

useful_potions.name: Restore Magicka
useful_potions.effect: Restore Magicka. Valuable potion; Frost Salts found on Frost Atronachs.

useful_potions.name: Cure Common Disease
useful_potions.effect: Cure Common Disease. Very cheap ingredients found everywhere in the wilderness.

useful_potions.name: Levitate
useful_potions.effect: Levitate. Essential for exploration; Trama Root is abundant in Ashlands.

useful_potions.name: Paralyze
useful_potions.effect: Paralyze. Extremely powerful in combat; cheap ingredients.

useful_potions.name: Fortify Strength
useful_potions.effect: Fortify Strength. High-value potion, Dreugh Wax is worth 100g.

useful_potions.name: Restore Health (expensive)
useful_potions.effect: Restore Health. Slightly more expensive ingredients but still common.

useful_potions.name: Invisibility
useful_potions.effect: Invisibility. Very high value potion due to Diamond ingredient.

useful_potions.name: Water Walking
useful_potions.effect: Water Walking. Useful for crossing water; ingredients are lightweight and common.

useful_potions.name: Resist Magicka
useful_potions.effect: Resist Magicka. Ash Salts has this as both 2nd and 4th effect; pair with any other Resist Magicka source.

useful_potions.name: Fortify Health (profitable)
useful_potions.effect: Fortify Health. Shalk Resin (50g) makes this a valuable potion to sell.

enchanting.mechanics: Enchanting applies permanent magical effects to items using trapped souls. Two methods: (1) Pay an NPC enchanter (guaranteed success, expensive), or (2) Self-enchant (free but risky). Self-enchant success formula: (0.75 + Fatigue%/2) * (1 - 0.5*isConstant) * (Enchant + Intelligence/5 + Luck/10 - 3*EnchantmentPoints). Key insight: enchantment points cost matters 3x as much as Enchant skill, which matters 5x as much as Intelligence, which matters 2x as much as Luck. Constant Effect enchantments require souls with 400+ capacity (Golden Saints, Ascended Sleepers). Best enchanting items: Ebony Staff (90 enchant points for weapons), Daedric Tower Shield (225 points for armor).

notable_items.name: Ebony Staff
notable_items.note: Highest weapon enchantment capacity - over 4x the next best staff

notable_items.name: Daedric Tower Shield
notable_items.note: Highest armor enchantment capacity, ideal for constant effect enchantments

notable_items.name: Azura's Star
notable_items.note: Reusable soul gem with 15000 capacity; not destroyed after use. Obtained from Azura's quest.

constant_effect_sources: Other large/rare creatures with 400+ soul value
